changed_functions.name: sub_1C005CAD4
key_changes: Removed the runtime selection via Feature_1230705977__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (sub_1c0009bbc) that chose between the new AfdCreateConnection (sub_1C005ABE4) and the legacy fallback AfdCreateConnection_Old (sub_1C0009CD8); the patched build always calls the new AfdCreateConnection (sub_1C005AB94)
changed_functions.security_notes: Staged WIL feature finalization. In the unpatched build the caller branches on the WIL feature gate Feature_1230705977__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage; when enabled it uses the new AfdCreateConnection, otherwise the legacy AfdCreateConnection_Old. Both functions allocate a thread-private connection object and neither acquires the endpoint spinlock. The patched build removes the gate and the legacy fallback. No lock removed, no TOCTOU, no UAF.

changed_functions.security_notes: Connection-establishment caller. Change is the finalization of Feature_1230705977 (removal of the feature gate and the legacy AfdCreateConnection_Old fallback). changed_functions.name: AfdCreateConnection_Old (sub_1C0009CD8)
key_changes: Legacy connection-creation fallback present only in the unpatched build; reached only when Feature_1230705977__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage returns disabled
Removed in the patched build as part of finalizing Feature_1230705977

Allocates a thread-private connection object via AfdAllocateConnection (0x1C0009D6B) and publishes it only at the end (mov [r12], rbx at 0x1C000A0AC); no endpoint spinlock is acquired, and none is required
changed_functions.security_notes: This is the legacy fallback, not a vulnerable function. It performs the same connection-creation work as the new AfdCreateConnection: allocate a fresh, thread-private connection object, initialize its fields, call IoCreateFile and ObReferenceObjectByHandle, manage the object refcount at [conn+0x30] with AfdCloseConnection on failure, and publish the object to the caller only at the end. Because the object is unreachable by other threads until publication, the absence of a lock is correct, not a TOCTOU/UAF. The only functional difference versus the new function is that IoCreateFile is called with a fixed DesiredAccess 0xC0100000 / Options 0x100, whereas the new function conditionally uses 0x2000000 / 0x101 based on endpoint flags [endpoint+0x10]&0x10000 or [endpoint+8]&0x8000000.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
0x1C0009D6B  call    AfdAllocateConnection   ; fresh thread-private object -> rbx
0x1C0009D70  mov     rbx, rax
0x1C0009D9C  call    cs:__imp_ObfReferenceObject
0x1C0009DAA  mov     [rbx+20h], rsi          ; init private object (no lock needed)
0x1C0009E55  mov     [rsp+130h+Options], 100h ; Options = 0x100 (fixed)
0x1C0009E66  mov     edx, 0C0100000h          ; DesiredAccess (fixed)
0x1C0009ED9  call    cs:__imp_IoCreateFile
0x1C0009F19  call    cs:__imp_ObReferenceObjectByHandle
0x1C0009F5C  mov     [rbx+18h], rax
0x1C0009F9F  lock xadd [rbx+30h], ecx        ; object refcount on error
0x1C0009FAC  call    AfdCloseConnection
0x1C000A0AC  mov     [r12], rbx               ; object first published here

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0073058
key_changes: Complete restructure; function re-matching artifact

summary: No security-relevant change. The diff is the finalization of several staged WIL (Windows Implementation Library) features in afd.sys, plus compiler churn. In the unpatched build, every connection-creation call site selects at runtime between a new AfdCreateConnection (sub_1C005ABE4) and a legacy fallback AfdCreateConnection_Old (sub_1C0009CD8) using the feature gate Feature_1230705977__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (sub_1c0009bbc, testing feature-state bit 0x10). The patched build removes the gate and the AfdCreateConnection_Old fallback and always calls the new AfdCreateConnection (sub_1C005AB94), which is functionally identical to the new function already present in the unpatched build. Neither connection-creation function acquires the endpoint spinlock, and none is required: both allocate a thread-private connection object via AfdAllocateConnection, initialize it locally, and publish it to the caller only at the end. There is no removed lock, no time-of-check/time-of-use race, and no use-after-free. The only genuine behavioral difference between the old and new functions is that the new one conditionally requests DesiredAccess 0x2000000 (MAXIMUM_ALLOWED) with Options 0x101 for the internal IoCreateFile when endpoint flags [endpoint+0x10]&0x10000 or [endpoint+8]&0x8000000 are set, instead of the fixed 0xC0100000 / 0x100; this has no demonstrable, reachable security impact. The other changed functions are likewise removals of WIL feature gates (Feature_623580472 in the AfdSan cancel/complete routines, Feature_2480880955 in a string helper, and Feature_3081238842 / Feature_3938434363 / Feature_2815687992 / Feature_2083252539 routing/drain gates) whose disabled paths reproduce the unpatched behavior, or compiler-optimization churn. Locking that earlier drafts attributed to the patch (AfdPreventUnbind/AfdReallowUnbind around Ob* calls and endpoint state) is already present identically in both builds.
